inputs.command:
cd vendor/get-anything-done && git add bin/ lib/ && git status --short && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(trace): phase 25 milestone A — trace schema v4 + hook handler + gad install hooks

Ships the hook-captured event stream foundation for trace schema v4.
Tasks 22-34 (install.js fix) and 25-01/02/03/04 (phase 25 milestone A).

22-34: install.js path resolution fix
  bin/install.js expected vendor/get-anything-done/get-anything-done/ — a
  subdirectory that only exists in npm-published tarballs, not local git
  checkouts. Before crashing it deleted .claude/commands/gad/ and
  .claude/hooks/statusline.js. Fix: existence check before copying the
  get-anything-done skill, and ensure the CHANGELOG/VERSION destination
  parent exists even when the skill copy was skipped. Verified by running
  the installer for --claude --cursor --codex --local to completion.

25-01: lib/trace-schema.cjs
  Event envelope + 4 event type factories + type guard + JSONL parser.
  Exports TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION=4, EVENT_TYPES constants, makeToolUseEvent,
  makeSkillInvocationEvent, makeSubagentSpawnEvent, makeFileMutationEvent,
  isTraceEvent, parseTraceEventsJsonl. Validated via node REPL smoke test.

25-03: lib/trace-truncate.cjs
  4 KB head+tail truncation helper (decision gad-60). truncateOutput splits
  at 2 KB head + 2 KB tail with a `... [truncated N bytes] ...` marker.
  truncateInputsObject applies the same to object field values. Multibyte-
  safe via Buffer-level slicing. Returns { value, truncated, originalBytes }.

25-02: bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs
  Claude Code PreToolUse / PostToolUse handler. Reads hook payload from
  stdin, finds project root by walking up for .planning/, reads the
  .planning/.trace-active-skill marker, detects skill transitions against
  .planning/.trace-last-skill, and writes trace events to
  .planning/.trace-events.jsonl.

  PreToolUse: checks for skill transitions only (lets the transition event
  land before the tool use that triggered the hook). PostToolUse: emits a
  tool_use event plus, when app
... [truncated 20 bytes] ...
spawn (for Task tool
  invocations) and/or file_mutation (for Write/Edit/NotebookEdit).

  Never blocks the main agent — all errors are logged to stderr and the
  hook exits 0. Verified end-to-end with a mock PostToolUse payload: the
  hook correctly appended a valid tool_use event to the JSONL file.

25-04: gad install hooks CLI subcommand
  New `gad install` + `gad uninstall` subcommands in bin/gad.cjs.
  `gad install hooks` writes PreToolUse + PostToolUse references to the
  framework-versioned handler path into .claude/settings.json (local) or
  ~/.claude/settings.json (--global). Merges into existing hooks — does
  not overwrite the user's other entries. Existing GAD trace entries are
  filtered out before re-adding so re-running the command is idempotent.
  `gad uninstall hooks` is the reverse operation.

  `gad install all --claude --cursor --codex --local` delegates to
  bin/install.js for the full framework install (wrapping the GSD-pattern
  installer). Locks in task 22-33 as satisfied by delegation.

  Verified: `gad install hooks` successfully merged the GAD trace handler
  into an existing .claude/settings.json that already had 8 hook entries;
  all pre-existing hooks preserved.

Smoke test verified:
  - trace-schema: event factories produce valid JSON, type guard round-
    trips, TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION=4
  - trace-truncate: small inputs passthrough, 5000-byte input truncates
    to 4127 bytes (head + marker + tail), truncated flag set
  - gad-trace-hook: appends valid tool_use event to
    .planning/.trace-events.jsonl when piped a mock PostToolUse payload
  - gad install hooks: merges into settings.json without overwriting

Phase 25 milestone A is complete. Milestone B (skill_invocation marker
contract + subagent_spawn wiring + file_mutation emission) still needs
documentation updates in skills to tell agents to write .trace-active-skill,
and the full end-to-end test of a real eval run against the new hooks.

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

Session 7: (1) Fixed YAML errors in create-skill and objective-eval-design SKILL.md — descriptions contained unquoted colons which js-yaml parses as key separators. Replaced plain scalar `description: ...` with folded block scalar `description: >- ...` which treats the body as opaque. Validated all 5 session-authored skills (create-skill, find-sprites, framework-upgrade, objective-eval-design, portfolio-sync) now parse clean. (2) Updated skills/create-skill/SKILL.md body to document the YAML frontmatter discipline so future skill authors use the folded block scalar pattern. (3) Ran vendor/get-anything-done/bin/install.js (the existing GSD-pattern installer supporting --claude --cursor --codex --all). Installer crashed partway through because its path resolution assumes an npm-published layout (expects vendor/get-anything-done/get-anything-done/). Before crash it successfully installed skills but deleted .claude/commands/gad/ and .claude/hooks/statusline.js. (4) Restored manually: copied commands into .claude/.cursor/.codex (60 each), copied 21 agents into .cursor/.codex/agents (already in .claude), copied 27 skills into .cursor/.codex/skills (already in .claude). Cleaned up .claude/get-anything-done/ installer artifact. (5) Final state: all three runtimes (.claude .cursor .codex) have 27 skills + 21 agents + 60 commands. Claude Code system reminder confirms all GAD skills load with no YAML errors. (6) Known: install.js has a path resolution bug for local (non-npm) checkouts — task 22-34 added to fix install.js, task 22-33 remains as the `gad install` CLI wrapper plan. NEXT SESSION: run /gad:execute-phase 25 or /gad:execute-phase 27 to ship trace v4 hooks or rubric work.</next-action>
inputs.new_string: <next-action>PHASE 22 SESSION 8: install.js fix + phase 25 milestone A shipped. (1) Task 22-34 fixed the install.js path bug (existence check for get-anything-done skill dir, mkdir parent for CHANGELOG/VERSION). Verified by running install for --claude --cursor --codex --local to clean completion across all three runtimes (60 commands + 21 agents + 27 skills each). (2) Phase 25 milestone A complete: lib/trace-schema.cjs (25-01, event envelope + 4 event type factories + parser), lib/trace-truncate.cjs (25-03, 4KB head+tail split with multibyte safety), bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs (25-02, Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse handler that reads stdin, walks up for project root, reads .trace-active-skill marker, detects transitions, emits tool_use/subagent_spawn/file_mutation events to .planning/.trace-events.jsonl), new `gad install hooks` and `gad install all` and `gad uninstall hooks` subcommands in bin/gad.cjs (25-04 — wires handler into .claude/settings.json preserving existing hook entries, delegates full framework install to bin/install.js). (3) Smoke test verified all 4 components: trace-schema factories produce valid JSON, truncate handles 5KB→4127 bytes correctly, hook handler appends valid events to JSONL when piped mock PostToolUse payload, install hooks merges into existing 8-entry settings.json without overwriting. Submodule commit 163e07f pushed. NEXT SESSION: phase 25 milestone B (document .trace-active-skill marker contract in skill docs, refine skill_invocation event emission with nested parent tracking, real eval run end-to-end test), OR phase 25 milestone C (framework versioning: gad version command + TRACE.json stamping), OR phase 27 track 3 (derived metrics from existing v3 data). All three can land in parallel.</next-action>

      <goal>Fix path resolution bug in bin/install.js for local git checkouts.</goal>

Each factory takes a plain options object and returns an envelope with ts (ISO8601), seq (monotonic int), type, and type-specific fields. Smoke-tested via node REPL: factories return valid events, type guard round-trips, parser handles invalid lines gracefully.</resolution>
      <keywords>trace-schema,v4,event-types,library</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="25-02" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Write the hook handler script at bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs that Claude Code invokes for PreToolUse/PostToolUse.</goal>
      <resolution>bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs reads hook payload from stdin, finds project root by walking up for .planning/, reads .planning/.trace-active-skill marker, detects transitions against .planning/.trace-last-skill file, and appends events to .planning/.trace-events.jsonl synchronously. PreToolUse emits only skill_invocation

l errors logged to stderr and hook exits 0. Verified end-to-end with a mock PostToolUse Bash payload: valid tool_use event appended correctly.</resolution>

      <keywords>hook-handler,claude-code,pretooluse,posttooluse,trace</keywords>
    </task>
    <task id="25-03" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>4 KB truncation helper with head+tail split for trace event outputs.</goal>
      <resolution>lib/trace-truncate.cjs exports truncateOutput({ value, truncated, originalBytes }) and truncateInputsObject helpers. Default cap 4096 bytes (decision gad-60), split 2048 head + 2048 tail joined by "... [truncated N bytes] ..." marker. Multibyte-safe via Buffer slicing. Returns the truncated flag for the caller to stamp on the event. Smoke-tested: 11-byte input passes through (truncated=false, 11 bytes); 5000-byte input truncates to 4127 bytes (truncated=true, original=5000).</resolution>
      <keywords>truncation,output-cap,4kb,trace</keywords>
    </task>

      <goal>`gad install hooks` CLI subcommand that wires the trace hook into Claude Code settings.json.</goal>
      <resolution>Added install + uninstall subcommands in bin/gad.cjs. `gad install hooks` (optional --global) merges a PreToolUse + PostToolUse entry pointing at the absolute path to bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs into .claude/settings.json (or ~/.claude/settings.json with --global). Merges into existing hooks — preserves all other entries. Idempotent: existing GAD trace entries (detected by "gad-trace-hook" substring) are filtered before re-adding. `gad uninstall hooks` is the reverse. `gad install all --claude --cursor --codex --local` delegates to bin/install.js as a wrapper satisfying task 22-33 via delegation. Verified: installed into a settings.json with 8 existing hook entries, all preserved, new GAD entries added to PreToolUse and PostToolUse.</resolution>
      <depends>25-02</depends>
      <keywords>cli,install-hooks,settings-json,gad-install</keywords>
    </task>

inputs.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: phase 25 milestones B/C/D + phase 27 track 3 — autonomous batch

Phase 25 Milestone B — skill marker docs:
  - 25-07: Added "Trace marker contract" section to skills/create-skill/SKILL.md
    explaining .planning/.trace-active-skill usage (write skill id at start,
    clear at end; hook handler detects transitions and emits events).
  - Updated plan-phase, execute-phase, verify-phase with trace marker notes.
  - 25-08/09/10 were already shipped in milestone A's gad-trace-hook.cjs:
    skill_invocation on .trace-active-skill transitions, subagent_spawn on
    Task tool calls, file_mutation on Write/Edit/NotebookEdit.

Phase 25 Milestone C — framework versioning:
  - 25-12: lib/framework-version.cjs + top-level `gad version [--json]`
    command. Reads package.json version + git rev-parse HEAD/branch/commit-ts
    from the GAD repo root. Returns { version, commit, branch, commit_ts, stamp }.
  - 25-13: evalTraceInit now stamps framework_version, framework_commit,
    framework_branch, framework_commit_ts, framework_stamp, and
    trace_schema_version=4 into every new TRACE.json.
  - 25-14: evalDiff (score-based) now checks framework_commit across two
    runs BEFORE showing the diff table. If they differ, emits a prominent
    "FRAMEWORK MISMATCH" warning explaining that score deltas may reflect
    framework changes, not agent changes. If either run is unstamped (pre-v4),
    warns that cross-version comparisons are unreliable.

Phase 25 Milestone D — site prebuild for trace v4:
  - 25-15: build-site-data.mjs extended to parse trace_schema_version,
    framework fields (version/commit/branch/ts/stamp), and traceEvents
    array from v4 TRACE.json files. All new fields on EvalRunRecord.
    Backwards-compatible: v3 runs get traceSchemaVersion=3, nulls for
    framework fields, null for traceEvents. Site rendering updates
    (tool use timeline component, subagent spawns section, framework
    badge on per-run page) deferred to the first session that has a
    real v4 run to render — the data pipeline is ready, the UI components
    come when there's data to show.

Phase 27 Track 3 — derived metrics:
  - 27-11: build-site-data.mjs computes derived metrics per run:
    divergence_score (|composite - human_review|), plan_adherence_delta
    (tasks_planned - tasks_completed), produced_artifact_density (artifacts
    per minute), total_commits, commit_discipline (task_id_commits /
    total_commits). Stubbed: tool_use_mix, skill_to_tool_ratio,
    subagent_utilization (require trace v4 events from phase 25 milestone A
    hooks running on a real eval).
  - EvalRunRecord type gains `derived` object exported in generated TS.
  - 27-12 (render derived metrics on per-run page) and 27-13/14 (charts)
    deferred to the next session's UI pass — the data pipeline is live,
    rendering follows.

Build output: 158 pages. Generated data now includes framework version
stamps and derived metrics for every implementation run.

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

inputs.command:
cd /c/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done && git add .planning/ evals/ site/ && git status --short | wc -l && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
eval: round 4 greenfield partial — rate-limited, preserved + findings doc

Round 4 v4 greenfield attempt #1. All three conditions (GAD v9, Bare v4,
Emergent v3) hit an account-level rate limit simultaneously at ~14 minutes.
None of the runs reached their gates. Partial data captured and honest.

Preserved runs:
- evals/escape-the-dungeon/v9/         — GAD, 81 tool_uses, full planning suite
  (ROADMAP/TASK-REGISTRY/STATE/DECISIONS/VERIFICATION — 7 phases, 23 tasks)
  + working dist (phase 01 verified PASS)
- evals/escape-the-dungeon-bare/v4/    — Bare, 45 tool_uses, flat worklog.md
  plan, 6 source files, manually built dist (54 KB)
- evals/escape-the-dungeon-emergent/v3/ — Emergent, 40 tool_uses, 7 inherited
  skills + 6 modular source files but NO main.ts — build fails objectively
  at vite link ("Rollup failed to resolve import /src/main.ts")

TRACE.json for each marked with:
- trace_schema_version: 4
- framework_stamp: v1.32.0+3ef0bb5 (first stamped runs!)
- timing.rate_limited: true + rate_limit_note
- human_review.notes explicitly says "DO NOT include in cross-round comparisons
  against completed runs"
- All scores and gate verdicts derived from observed partial state (not
  hallucinated — computed from what actually landed on disk)

Two new dists copied into site/public/playable/:
- escape-the-dungeon/v9/     — GAD title screen (only gate-verifiable work)
- escape-the-dungeon-bare/v4/ — bare scaffold + partial combat UI (manually
  built because agent was cut off before running npm run build)
- escape-the-dungeon-emergent/v3/ — NOT PLAYABLE (build fails; entry missing)

FINDINGS-2026-04-09-round-4-partial.md:
- Full write-up of what each condition accomplished in 14 minutes
- Cross-condition planning differential table (GAD 23 tasks planned vs Bare 10
  vs Emergent 0) — the planning overhead of GAD is clearly visible even in
  the truncated window
- Architectural differential: GAD subdirectory split, Bare flat file, Emergent
  state-composition influenced module structure
- Explicit list of what this data does NOT tell us (freedom hypothesis neither
  confirmed nor refuted, v4 still untested, trace events not yet processed)
- Retry plan: serial execution when rate limit resets

Decisions:
- gad-62: Parallel eval runs share one account rate bucket. Serial execution
  is the new default. Parallel requires documented per-run budget.
- gad-63: Rate-limited runs are preserved as data points but explicitly
  excluded from cross-round quality comparisons. Filter key is
  timing.rate_limited === true.

Site prebuild: 23 runs (was 20), 10 playable builds (was 8). New per-run
pages auto-generated for v9 / v4 / v3. New finding doc auto-indexed at
/findings/2026-04-09-round-4-partial. No UI code changes this commit — just
data. Filter-out-rate-limited logic for Graphs + Results is a follow-up task
for the next session.

Framework: v1.32.0+3ef0bb5 (trace hooks installed, framework versioning live).
These are the first three TRACE.json files stamped with framework_commit —
cross-version comparisons from here on can distinguish "agent improved" from
"framework changed."

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

inputs.command:
cd /c/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done && git add site/ && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(site): filter rate-limited runs from cross-round comparisons (gad-63)

New isRateLimited(run) helper + comparableRuns() wrapper in lib/eval-data.ts.
Applied consistently across three surfaces:

- Graphs.tsx scatter + bar chart: runsWithScores() now excludes
  isRateLimited runs from both the composite×human review scatter and the
  per-run bar chart. Freedom-hypothesis signal stays clean.
- Results.tsx cards: DISPLAY_RUNS filters isRateLimited in addition to
  requiring humanReview.score. The grid of per-run result cards no longer
  shows the round 4 partial runs.
- Per-run page: new amber "rate-limited · excluded from cross-round
  comparisons" badge in the header row, plus a full callout block above
  the divergence callout that explains the exclusion, cites gad-63, and
  links to /findings/2026-04-09-round-4-partial. Divergence callout now
  suppressed when rateLimited to avoid double-warning overload.

Runs are still preserved on disk and reachable at /runs/<project>/<version>
for archival purposes — only the aggregation surfaces filter them out.
The rate_limit_note from TRACE.json timing is surfaced verbatim in the
per-run callout so readers see exactly why the run was flagged.

Build verified clean: 23 per-run pages still generate (8 comparable runs
filtered from the cards + 3 rate-limited now hidden from scatter +
20 historical + 3 rate-limited = all 23 still have pages, just not on
aggregate surfaces).

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push origin main 2>&1 | tail -3

inputs.old_string:
<decision id="gad-64">
    <title>API-interrupted runs are a separate failure category from rate-limited runs</title>
inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-65">
    <title>Compound-Skills Hypothesis — emergent workflow as a skill-growth framework tailored to a specific project domain</title>
    <summary>The emergent eval condition is not just "inherit some skills and don't regress." It is a testable hypothesis about knowledge compounding: **as an emergent agent iterates on the same project domain across rounds, its inherited skill library should grow more specialized to that domain, and the game it produces should improve in quality and requirements-alignment per round more than bare produces by starting fresh.** The craftsman metaphor: a blacksmith who learns one skill per hour and authors one skill per hour becomes a master of the specific kind of blade they keep forging. The test signals: (1) human review score increases monotonically across emergent versions; (2) inherited skill count grows across rounds; (3) the ratio of project-specific skills to generic methodology skills trends toward specific; (4) time-to-working-game decreases across rounds despite the requirements getting stricter. Round 4 emergent v4 produced the first observed full inheritance → apply → evolve → document cycle (authored dom-over-kaplay + pressure-forge-coupling and deprecated kaplay-scene-pattern). The next emergent run (v5) is the first real test of whether v4's learnings actually help. DISTINCT from the freedom hypothesis (bare beats GAD on creative implementation) — CSH is specifically about whether the emergent mechanism produces compounding returns over time, independent of the bare vs GAD framing.</summary>
    <impact>Phase 27 rubric adds a sixth dimension for emergent runs: skill_inheritance_effectiveness (weight 0.20), which directly scores whether a run productively applied inherited skills, evolved them, and authored new skills worth inheriting. Site's /projects/escape-the-dungeon-emergent page gains a skill lineage view showing which skills each run inherited, evolved, deprecated, and authored. Findings writeups track CSH metrics separately from freedom-hypothesis metrics. Every emergent run's TRACE.json should capture inherited_skill_count, authored_skill_count, deprecated_skill_count, and (optionally) a specificity_score measuring how tailored the authored skills are to the roguelike domain vs generic workflow.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-64">
    <title>API-interrupted runs are a separate failure category from rate-limited runs</title>

inputs.old_string: <next-action>PHASE 22 SESSION 8: install.js fix + phase 25 milestone A shipped. (1) Task 22-34 fixed the install.js path bug (existence check for get-anything-done skill dir, mkdir parent for CHANGELOG/VERSION). Verified by running install for --claude --cursor --codex --local to clean completion across all three runtimes (60 commands + 21 agents + 27 skills each). (2) Phase 25 milestone A complete: lib/trace-schema.cjs (25-01, event envelope + 4 event type factories + parser), lib/trace-truncate.cjs (25-03, 4KB head+tail split with multibyte safety), bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs (25-02, Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse handler that reads stdin, walks up for project root, reads .trace-active-skill marker, detects transitions, emits tool_use/subagent_spawn/file_mutation events to .planning/.trace-events.jsonl), new `gad install hooks` and `gad install all` and `gad uninstall hooks` subcommands in bin/gad.cjs (25-04 — wires handler into .claude/settings.json preserving existing hook entries, delegates full framework install to bin/install.js). (3) Smoke test verified all 4 components: trace-schema factories produce valid JSON, truncate handles 5KB→4127 bytes correctly, hook handler appends valid events to JSONL when piped mock PostToolUse payload, install hooks merges into existing 8-entry settings.json without overwriting. Submodule commit 163e07f pushed. NEXT SESSION: phase 25 milestone B (document .trace-active-skill marker contract in skill docs, refine skill_invocation event emission with nested parent tracking, real eval run end-to-end test), OR phase 25 milestone C (framework versioning: gad version command + TRACE.json stamping), OR phase 27 track 3 (derived metrics from existing v3 data). All three can land in parallel.</next-action>
inputs.new_string: <next-action>PHASE 23 ACTIVE (round 4 greenfield) + PHASE 27 track 1 shipped. Round 4 results: Bare v5 + Emergent v4 shipped complete playable games against v4 pressure-oriented requirements, GAD v10 api-interrupted at phases 01+02 completion (55 tool_uses for planning + data layer, no scene implementation reached). Freedom hypothesis holds under v4. Emergent v4 authored 2 new skills (dom-over-kaplay, pressure-forge-coupling) and a CHANGELOG — first observed full inheritance→apply→evolve→document cycle. Phase 27 track 1 (rubric) shipped session 13: lib/rubric-schema.cjs + human_review_rubric blocks in 3 greenfield gad.json files (emergent gets a 6th dimension `skill_inheritance_effectiveness` as the compound-skills hypothesis test signal) + prebuild parser handles both legacy single-score and rubric formats via normalizeHumanReviewPrebuild + `gad eval review --rubric '<json>'` CLI extension + RubricRadar SVG chart component rendered on per-run pages when rubric is populated. Decision gad-65 pins the Compound-Skills Hypothesis. Skill inheritance lineage table live on /projects/escape-the-dungeon-emergent showing per-run newly-authored vs carried-over skills. Task 21-23b queued for GAD v11 retry after 529 investigation. NEXT SESSION USER WORK: play Bare v5 and Emergent v4 on the deployed site, submit rubric review via `gad eval review escape-the-dungeon-bare v5 --rubric '{"playability":0.8,...}' --notes "..."`. Then NEXT AGENT SESSION: investigate HTTP 529 pattern, retry GAD v11, regenerate site data, push.</next-action>

feat: phase 27 track 1 (rubric) + gad-65 compound-skills hypothesis + lineage view

score human_review field. Runs in parallel with the round 4 human review work.

27-01: lib/rubric-schema.cjs
  DEFAULT_DIMENSIONS for escape-the-dungeon family (playability 0.30, ui_polish
  0.20, mechanics_implementation 0.20, ingenuity_requirement_met 0.20,
  stability 0.10 — weights sum to 1.0). Exports emptyRubric(), computeAggregate(),
  isLegacyReview(), normalizeHumanReview(), validateRubric(). Legacy single-score
  runs normalized via synthetic `overall` dimension so the site handles both
  formats without branching in UI code.

27-02: human_review_rubric blocks in 3 gad.json files
  escape-the-dungeon (gad): 5 dimensions, default weights
  escape-the-dungeon-bare: 5 dimensions, default weights
  escape-the-dungeon-emergent: 6 dimensions — adds skill_inheritance_effectiveness
    (weight 0.20) as the direct test signal for the compound-skills hypothesis.
    Other weights rebalanced: playability 0.25, ui_polish 0.15, mechanics 0.15,
    ingenuity 0.15, stability 0.10, skill_inheritance 0.20.

27-03: Prebuild parses both formats
  scripts/build-site-data.mjs new normalizeHumanReviewPrebuild() handles legacy
  {score, notes} AND new {rubric_version, dimensions, aggregate_score} shapes.
  New humanReviewNormalized field on EvalRunRecord — consistent shape for the
  site. Type extends generated EvalProjectMeta with humanReviewRubric dimensions.

27-04: gad eval review --rubric extension
  Existing `gad eval review` command extended with --rubric flag accepting a JSON
  string of dimension→score pairs. Validates against the project's declared
  rubric dimensions in gad.json, computes weighted aggregate, writes to
  TRACE.json with rubric_version stamp. Legacy --scor
... [truncated 1714 bytes] ...
 test.

Skill inheritance lineage view on /projects/escape-the-dungeon-emergent
  New section only rendered for workflow=emergent projects. Table per run
  showing skills in game/.planning/skills/, total count, and net change
  (newly authored vs carried over vs dropped) relative to the previous run.
  Newly authored skills badged with ✨ amber. Direct visual read of the
  compound-skills hypothesis signal — you can see the library growing.

21-23b queued: GAD v11 retry after 529 investigation
  Task added to TASK-REGISTRY.xml. Prerequisite: diagnose the HTTP 529 pattern
  we hit twice on GAD v10 (tool_uses 18 and 55). Possible mitigations include
  retry-with-backoff in the eval runner, shorter API invocations for snapshot +
  planning XML writes, or just "the API was temporarily overloaded that hour".
  Only retry v11 once we have a conclusion.

BONUS fix (unplanned but found during this session):
  .claude/settings.json had 7 hook references to files that don't exist
  (gad-context-monitor.js, gad-phase-boundary.sh, gad-check-update.js,
  gad-session-state.sh, gad-prompt-guard.js, gad-validate-commit.sh,
  gad-statusline.js). These were written by install.js during an earlier
  session but the files themselves never landed. Claude Code logged a
  "PostToolUse:Edit hook error" (and Read, TaskUpdate, Grep) on every tool
  call because the hook commands failed with ENOENT. Fixed by removing
  the dead references — settings.json now only points at:
    - .claude/hooks/context-monitor.js (pre-existing, OK)
    - .claude/hooks/tool-trace.js (pre-existing, OK)
    - vendor/get-anything-done/bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs (phase 25, mine, OK)
  Also removed the statusLine config pointing at missing gad-statusline.js.
  Verified all remaining hook files resolve and exit 0 on empty stdin.

STATE.xml updated: phase 23 active, round 4 results summarized, next-action
points at user playing Bare v5 and Emergent v4 to submit rubric reviews.

Made-with: Claude Opus 4.6

      "filename": "find-sprites.md",
      "content": "---
name: find-sprites
description: Source visual assets (sprites, icons, tilesets, portraits) for a game or UI-heavy build in a way that yields a coherent, intentional look instead of a debug-console aesthetic. Use this skill when you need art for rooms, entities, UI controls, HP bars, spell icons, status effects, or any other visual element; when the build is failing its UI-quality gate because it looks unintentional; or when you're about to fall back to raw ASCII/text UI. The goal is "looks like someone designed it" — not photorealism, not 1:1 with AAA games, but internally consistent and readable.
---

# find-sprites

Agents left to their own devices tend to ship raw text UI, emoji salad, or inconsistent asset mixtures. This skill exists because the `ui-quality` gate consistently fails when asset sourcing is treated as an afterthought. Source assets deliberately, in preference order, and commit to one visual direction.

## Preference order

Work down this list. Only drop to the next tier if the current tier cannot cover your need.

### Tier 1 — Installable icon and sprite packages

Lightweight, well-typed, import cleanly. Best for UI iconography, status effects, resource icons, and action buttons.

| Package | Use for |
|---|---|
| `lucide-react` / `lucide` | UI icons, buttons, controls, status (~1500 icons) |
| `@iconify/react` / `@iconify-json/*` | Broadest set — game-icons pack, emojione, twemoji, etc. |
| `@tabler/icons` | Clean outline style, good for minimalist UI |
| `heroicons` | Simple outline + solid, Tailwind-friendly |

For game iconography specifically, **`@iconify-json/game-icons`** (Delapouite/Lorc) is the single highest-leverage install:

```sh
npm install @iconify/react @iconify-json/game-icons
```

```tsx
import { Icon } from "@iconify/react";
<Icon icon="game-icons:crossed-swor
... [truncated 21374 bytes] ...
ntentionally.** Only acceptable for background panels or temporary placeholders you will replace before shipping.

### Tier 5 — Raw ASCII / text-only

This is a gate failure. Do not ship text-only UI. If you are tempted to, go back to Tier 1 and install the game-icons pack — it takes 30 seconds.

## Coherence over polish

One consistent visual direction beats a mix of high-quality assets from different styles. A game built entirely from Kenney's 1-bit pack looks intentional. A game mixing hand-drawn portraits, pixel monsters, and flat-vector UI icons looks broken — even if each individual asset is beautiful.

Before sourcing anything, decide:

1. **Perspective** — top-down, side-view, or UI-only?
2. **Fidelity** — pixel, vector, or illustrated?
3. **Palette** — dark fantasy, bright cartoon, monochrome?

Then source only assets matching those three answers. If a must-have asset doesn't match, either adapt it (recolor, resize) or redraw the rest of your direction to match it.

## Licensing

Always check the license. CC0 is the simplest — no attribution required, no restrictions. CC-BY requires attribution (include the author in `LICENSES.md`). Avoid CC-BY-NC (non-commercial) and CC-BY-SA (share-alike viral) unless you've confirmed your project's distribution model is compatible. Never ship assets of unknown provenance.

## Failure modes

- **Mixing styles.** Pixel + vector + illustrated = incoherent. Pick one.
- **Installing an icon pack and never using it.** The install is the easy part. Actually replace placeholder text with `<Icon />` in every UI surface.
- **Downloading a tileset without a license check.** Ship-blocker if the license forbids the use.
- **Deferring art to "after mechanics work."** The UI-quality gate is scored on what actually ships. An unpolished build with working mechanics still fails the gate.
- **Using emoji as the entire UI.** Reads as a debugger, not a game.

inputs.old_string: <decision id="gad-68">
inputs.new_string:
<decision id="gad-71">
    <title>data/ JSON pseudo-database for hand-curated site content</title>
    <summary>User proposal: "we might want to have a pseudo database that is json some json that gets stored in the repo and we use that to collect all the static data we need for the site and operate like it is a database, just in repo as dont expect customers and sensitive information at all." Formalize the pattern: `vendor/get-anything-done/data/*.json` files are the authoritative source of hand-curated content for the site. The prebuild script (`site/scripts/build-site-data.mjs`) reads them at build time and emits typed TypeScript via `site/lib/*.generated.ts`. The site never runs queries at request time — every page is SSG, every data file is read once at prebuild, types are statically exported. This is distinct from `.planning/` XML (which is agent-authored process state like phase/task/decision) and from `evals/<project>/<version>/TRACE.json` (which is eval-run output). `data/` is specifically for curated knowledge that doesn't belong in either of those two places: open questions for the public, observed bugs across eval runs, glossary terms, user-workflow assumptions, content packs extracted for gad-66, human-review tracking metadata, etc. Security posture is explicit: this is a static site with no customers, no authentication, no PII, no secrets. JSON-in-git is appropriate because we have no need for ACID, transactions, or concurrent writers — one human edits files in a branch, the prebuild regenerates, the site ships. If we ever need any of those properties (we don't expect to), the migration path is clear: JSON schemas become table definitions, the prebuild becomes a seed script.</summary>
    <impact>(1) Create `vendor/get-anything-done/data/` with an initial set of files: `open-questions.json`, `bugs.json`, `glossary.json`, `assumptions.json`. (2) Extend `build-site-data.mjs` with a new section that reads each data file, validates shape, and writes corresponding TS exports. (3) New site routes backed by these files: `/questions` (open-questions.json), eventually `/glossary`, `/assumptions`. (4) Every file gets a top-level `_schema` field documenting its shape + a `_last_updated` field maintained by hand. (5) When a new hand-curated data type is needed, the pattern is: add `data/<name>.json` → add a reader to `build-site-data.mjs` → add a typed export to `lib/<name>.generated.ts` → build a consuming page. No new runtime services, no servers, no API routes.</impact>
  </decision>

  <decision id="gad-68">

No new runtime services, no servers, no API routes.</impact>","+  </decision>","+","   <decision id="gad-68">","     <title>Emergent-evolution working hypothesis — skill-evolution + emergent projects + tailored merging</title>","     <summary>Synthesis of the freedom hypothesis (gad-36), compound-skills hypothesis (gad-65), and the consistent GAD-framework hiccups observed across rounds 1-4 into a single working hypothesis. Statement: "A coding agent given blank planning artifacts, requirements, a task, and the ability to (a) create new skills, (b) merge similar skills, (c) find related skills to help, plus GAD-provided foundational skills to merge against, will produce better work than framework-constrained or bare conditions over time — because projects are themselves emergent, and skills must be tailored to the specific project continually and merged with similar foundational skills to maintain consistency." The craftsman/lifter metaphor: a master lifter varies exercise and lowers weight to stay sharp, the craftsman returns to fundamentals to maintain form — depending on the goal, the agent should tailor to the project while rehearsing fundamentals. This aligns with what the original Ralph Wiggum loop creator and the repomirror creators have written about as well. The GAD framework's role is NOT to prescribe process — it's to provide the foundational skill pool to merge against and an evolution substrate for project-tailored skills to form on top. This hypothesis is the research goal of the emergent eval condition; freedom hypothesis tests bare vs framework on a single round while this hypothesis tests whether GAD-with-evolution compounds over many rounds into project mastery. Distinct from vanilla compound-skills: this adds the merging + foundational-rehearsal dimensions and explicitly names GAD's role as a skill pool rather than a process prescription.</summary>"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}

{
  "_schema": {
    "file": "data/bugs.json",
    "purpose": "Bugs observed in eval runs. Tracked over time across versions so regressions / fixes can be compared.",
    "written_by": "human (after playtest)",
    "read_by": "site prebuild + site pages",
    "shape": {
      "bugs[]": {
        "id": "stable kebab-case slug",
        "title": "short description",
        "project": "eval project id",
        "version": "version tag where observed (e.g. v5)",
        "observed_on": "ISO date",
        "severity": "low | medium | high | critical",
        "status": "open | fixed | wontfix | reproduced-elsewhere",
        "description": "what happens",
        "expected": "what should happen",
        "reproduction": "steps to reproduce",
        "related_requirement": "Rvx.yy tag from requirements addendum, optional",
        "related_runs": "array of other {project, version} where the same bug was observed, optional"
      }
    }
  },
  "_last_updated": "2026-04-09",
  "bugs": [
    {
      "id": "rune-forge-same-rune-twice",
      "title": "Rune forge allows same rune twice in a single spell, boosts affinity twice",
      "project": "escape-the-dungeon-bare",
      "version": "v5",
      "observed_on": "2026-04-09",
      "severity": "medium",
      "status": "open",
      "description": "In the rune forge, the player can select the same rune twice as ingredients for a single spell. When crafted, the affinity gain for that rune is applied twice as if two distinct rune slots were consumed. The resulting spell also treats the duplicate as a meaningful second ingredient.",
      "expected": "Selecting a rune that is already in the current spell's ingredient list should either reject the second click or toggle that rune off. Affinity gain should be per-unique-rune-slot. Duplicate runes across DIFFERENT spells in the player's spellbook remain fine and are explicitly allowed.",
      "reproduction": "Open forge. Select Fire rune. Select Fire rune again. Craft. Observe that Fire affinity increases by 2x the normal amo
... [truncated 774 bytes] ...
clears stale notifications.",
      "reproduction": "Play until a notification appears. Do not dismiss. Start a new game. Observe notification still overlays the new session.",
      "related_requirement": "R-v5.10",
      "related_runs": []
    },
    {
      "id": "continue-after-death-greyed-out",
      "title": "Continue button disabled after death — no save checkpoints",
      "project": "escape-the-dungeon-emergent",
      "version": "v4",
      "observed_on": "2026-04-09",
      "severity": "high",
      "status": "open",
      "description": "On player death the game ends and Continue is no longer selectable. No automatic checkpoint appears to have been created during the run.",
      "expected": "Room-clear or floor-clear creates a checkpoint. On death, Continue loads the most recent checkpoint.",
      "reproduction": "Start new game. Die in any encounter. Return to title. Continue is greyed out.",
      "related_requirement": "R-v5.09",
      "related_runs": []
    },
    {
      "id": "glitchy-button-click-redraws",
      "title": "Glitchy redraws on button clicks across all round-4 builds",
      "project": "escape-the-dungeon",
      "version": "v10",
      "observed_on": "2026-04-09",
      "severity": "medium",
      "status": "open",
      "description": "UI visibly glitches/flickers on button clicks as if full per-tick redraws are running. Observed consistently across GAD v9, GAD v10, Bare v5, and Emergent v4.",
      "expected": "Event-driven rendering only. No redraw loops firing at a fixed rate regardless of state changes. React/DOM reconciles on state change; requestAnimationFrame only runs when animation is active.",
      "reproduction": "Click any menu button in any round-4 build. Observe brief UI flicker.",
      "related_requirement": "R-v5.21",
      "related_runs": [
        { "project": "escape-the-dungeon", "version": "v9" },
        { "project": "escape-the-dungeon-bare", "version": "v5" },
        { "project": "escape-the-dungeon-emergent", "version": "v4" }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

    {
      "id": "csh-vs-freedom-which-wins-over-time",
      "title": "Does compounding skill inheritance (CSH) eventually beat raw freedom (freedom hypothesis) across many rounds?",
      "category": "hypothesis",
      "status": "open",
      "priority": "high",
      "context": "Round 4 produced a provocative finding: Emergent v4 scored 0.885 (with the 6-dim rubric including skill_inheritance_effectiveness), Bare v5 scored 0.805 (5-dim rubric), and GAD v10 was api-interrupted. On shared rubric dimensions (playability, ui_polish, mechanics, ingenuity, stability) Emergent beat Bare on playability and UI polish, tied on mechanics/ingenuity/stability. The freedom hypothesis (gad-36) says bare beats framework on creative output. The compound-skills hypothesis (gad-65) says emergent-with-evolution compounds over rounds. Round 4 may be the first evidence that CSH is overtaking freedom — BUT it's one round, rubric reweighting matters, a
... [truncated 4755 bytes] ...
ifferent navigation priority.",
      "related_decisions": ["gad-68"],
      "related_requirements": [],
      "opened_on": "2026-04-09",
      "resolved_on": null,
      "resolution": null
    },
    {
      "id": "is-our-rubric-worth-its-ceiling",
      "title": "Is the 6th skill_inheritance_effectiveness dimension on emergent an unfair boost vs the 5-dim bare/gad rubric?",
      "category": "evaluation",
      "status": "discussing",
      "priority": "medium",
      "context": "Emergent v4 aggregate: 0.885. Bare v5 aggregate: 0.805. The 0.08 delta maps almost exactly to emergent's 6th-dimension contribution (skill_inheritance_effectiveness 0.95 * 0.20 weight = +0.19 against bare's absent-dimension 0). Shared-dimension comparison: emergent +0.18 playability, +0.10 ui_polish, tie on mechanics/ingenuity/stability. Is that 6th dimension double-counting emergent's inheritance advantage? Arguably yes — CSH is being tested by the existence of that dimension. Counter: without it, there's no way to score whether inheritance is actually working, which is the whole point of the emergent condition.",
      "related_decisions": ["gad-65"],
      "related_requirements": [],
      "opened_on": "2026-04-09",
      "resolved_on": null,
      "resolution": null
    },

      "category": "site",
      "status": "open",
      "priority": "high",
      "context": "Current nav already has 11 items (GAD, Lineage, Methodology, Rubric, Results, Graphs, Videos, Catalog, Findings, Planning, and now + Rubric). Tasks 86/87/88/95 will add more. Task 84 plans dropdown grouping + keyword search before more pages ship. Open: what is the grouping that makes sense to a first-time visitor vs a returning researcher?",
      "related_decisions": [],
      "related_requirements": [],
      "opened_on": "2026-04-09",
      "resolved_on": null,
      "resolution": null
    }
  ]
}

ing | null {

/**
 * True when the run hit a rate limit before completing. Rate-limited runs
 * are preserved as data points but excluded from cross-round comparisons
 * per decision gad-63.
 */
export function isRateLimited(r: EvalRunRecord): boolean {
  if (!r.timing) return false;
  const flag = (r.timing as Record<string, unknown>).rate_limited;
  return flag === true;
}

/**
 * True when the run was interrupted by an Anthropic API error (e.g. HTTP 529
 * overloaded_error). Different cause than rate limit — infrastructure issue
 * rather than account cap — but same exclusion policy per decision gad-64.
 */
export function isApiInterrupted(r: EvalRunRecord): boolean {
  if (!r.timing) return false;
  const flag = (r.timing as Record<string, unknown>).api_interrupted;
  return flag === true;
}

/**
 * True when the run was interrupted for ANY reason (rate limit OR API error).
 * Use this as the default filter for comparison surfaces.
 */
export function isInterrupted(r: EvalRunRecord): boolean {
  return isRateLimited(r) || isApiInterrupted(r);
}

/**
 * Returns only runs suitable for cross-round comparison — completed runs
 * with a scored composite, no interruption flags.
 */
export function comparableRuns(runs: EvalRunRecord[] = EVAL_RUNS): EvalRunRecord[] {
  return runs.filter((r) => !isInterrupted(r) && r.scores.composite != null);
}

inputs.content:
# Programmatic-Evaluation Gaps Audit

**Owner:** GAD framework maintainers.
**Anchor decision:** [gad-69](DECISIONS.xml) — every eval metric must answer "can this be collected programmatically?" before "how do we score it?". Minimize agent-self-report and human-judgment dependencies.
**Scope:** every field currently captured in `TRACE.json`, `human_review_rubric`, `derived` metrics, and hook output.
**Status:** living document. Updated each round. Ranked by **severity** (how much judgment we currently rely on) × **feasibility** (how mechanically checkable the signal actually is).
**Last updated:** 2026-04-09.

---

## Methodology

For every existing eval signal, answer three questions:

1. **Current source:** where does this number come from today? (TRACE.json field written by the agent, human rubric, derived formula over traces, hook event)
2. **Trust model:** is it agent-self-report, human-judgment, tool-observed, or computed-deterministic?
3. **Programmatic alternative:** if it's self-report or human, what tooling could collect the same signal objectively?

A gap exists when the answer to (2) is self-report or human AND the answer to (3) is "yes, this is mechanically checkable with reasonable effort."

---

## Ranked gaps

### G1 · Gate-pass verdict (G1 game-loop, G2 forge, G3 UI, G4 pressure)

- **Current source:** `requirementCoverage.gate_failed` boolean + `gate_notes` string, written by the agent self-reporting in TRACE.json, optionally reviewed by a human.
- **Trust model:** agent self-report, occasionally refuted by human playtest.
- **Programmatic alternative:** a **Playwright smoke-test harness** run against the preserved build in `apps/portfolio/public/evals/<project>/<version>/`. G1 game-loop is testable (title → new game → enter room → find exit → navigate → encounter → resolve → return). G2 forge is testable (open forge → select 2 runes → craft → see new spell in inventory). G3 UI is partially testable (assert no raw ASCII, assert bars rendered, assert icons present by CSS c
... [truncated 8423 bytes] ...
 coverage | high | medium | phase 25 milestone E (new) |
| G3 Build/test exit code | high | high | phase 25 milestone D follow-up |
| G6 Requirement coverage counts | high | medium | phase 27 track 2 (merge with G1) |
| G11 Skill-inheritance hygiene | high | high (hygiene) | phase 27 new task |
| G5 Commit rhythm sharpening | low | medium | polish |
| G7 Subagent spawn audit | medium | high | phase 25 milestone B verify |
| G8 Asset sourcing heuristic | medium | high | phase 25 milestone D follow-up |
| G9 Notification persistence | medium | high | phase 27 track 2 |
| G10 Per-tick redraw detection | medium | medium | phase 27 track 2 |
| G12 Consistency cross-check | low | high | nice-to-have |
| G4 Tool-use mix | — | — | already good, keep as reference pattern |

## Recommended next actions

1. **Pick G1 + G3 as the two load-bearing wins.** Together they replace the two flimsiest self-report signals (gate verdict and build-works) with deterministic outputs. Both are high feasibility. This is the highest ROI.
2. **Add G11's hygiene check as the CSH-measurement upgrade.** We already have the hook-captured data; we just need a checker that walks inherited-skill files + mutation events + CHANGELOG and reports hygiene %.
3. **Defer G2 until v5 requirements promotion lands** — expected triggers change with v5, no point building a checker against a moving target.
4. **G4 is the reference pattern.** Every new programmatic metric should follow its shape: trace events in, deterministic derivation out, typed field on EvalRunRecord, rendered on the per-run page with a data-provenance caption ("this score reads X from TRACE.json derived.foo").

## Data provenance convention (follows from G4)

Every metric rendered on the site must be annotated with:
- where the number comes from (TRACE field or derived formula)
- who produced it (agent self-report, hook, prebuild computation, human review)
- whether it's programmatic or not (badge)

This is the `data provenance` view the user asked for on the /roadmap page (task 88).

inputs.new_string: <next-action>BARE V5 PLAYTESTED + EMERGENT V4 RESCORED UPWARD + PROGRAMMATIC-EVAL GAPS AUDIT DONE. Bare v5 rubric: 0.805 (playability 0.70, ui_polish 0.85, mechanics 0.90, ingenuity 0.95, stability 0.55) — highest ingenuity of any round-4 run, multi-enemy encounters, great forge UI, affinity mechanic loved, pressure mechanics landed (subtle hints on map appreciated), but lacks targeting (wants UO-style rule-based combat), affinity reward unclear, no visual map + player location, unclear player/enemy identity in encounters, glitchy redraws across ALL round-4 builds, rune forge bug. Emergent v4 RESCORED 0.805 → 0.885: user beat floor 1 by skipping combat, gathering mana, crafting spells, then fighting warden fresh. Floor is beatable — "fantastic representation of what a game should FEEL like." Playability 0.65 → 0.88, ingenuity 0.80 → 0.95. V5 addendum extended with 9 more requirements (R-v5.13..21): rule-based combat a la Unicorn Overlord, entity-trait action policies for enemies AND NPCs, real-time game-time (1hr=1day, remove ticks), affinity reward loop, central visual nav map with player token, UO-style encounter visuals, spell-mixing-spells with procedural-but-semantic naming, rune uniqueness within a single spell, event-driven rendering to kill per-tick redraw glitches. glossary + Term tooltip + gad-72/73/74 + GAPS de-Playwright'd

User unblock: "im reading the questions and its difficult to know
everything we are building all at once and I need some quick
reference" — ship the glossary system end-to-end.

Ships:
- data/glossary.json seeded with 27 terms across 7 categories
  (hypothesis, workflow, evaluation, framework, game-design,
  process, infra). CSH, freedom hypothesis, emergent-evolution,
  emergent/bare/GAD workflows, greenfield/brownfield, gate,
  rubric, composite, ingenuity, forge, affinity, pressure,
  trace v4, hooks, rate-limited, api-interrupted, run, round,
  fundamental-skills, merge-skill, programmatic-eval,
  pseudo-database, Ralph Wiggum loop, planning loop.
- /glossary route: grouped-by-category cards with short +
  full definitions, aliases, related decisions, cross-linked
  related terms. Category jump nav at top.
- Term component (server-side, native <abbr title>): wraps
  inline terms anywhere on the site with a dotted underline
  + hover tooltip + click-through to the /glossary anchor.
  No client JS, no runtime fetch.
- /questions retrofit: hero copy uses <Term> for "hypotheses"
  and "rubric" as the first retrofit example.
- Nav gains /glossary link (13 items now — IA refactor is
  critical, tracked as task 84).

New decisions:
- gad-72 rounds framework: rounds are defined by requirements
  version; differing eval versions exist within a round;
  progressively more complex; earlier rounds can be revisited
  with new hypotheses; a new hypothesis can start a new round
  against any version. "Does codex cli build like claude code
  against v4" is a valid new round, not a new game version.
- gad-73 fundamental skills triumvirate: find-skills +
  merge-skill + create-skill are GAD's foundational set. The
  meta-layer of the in-game rune/spell merge mechanic.
- gad-74 GAD's real value prop: task management at scale in
  repo + scientific skill/workflow evaluation, NOT "ship
  software faster." The landing must reframe.

Question resolutions + additions:
- Resolved: content-pack-injection-baseline — goes into its
  own eval track as a content-driven derivative hypothesis.
- New: fundamental-skills-triumvirate (audit current skills,
  build what's missing).
- New: gad-value-prop-landing-page-framing.
- New: skill-security-model (effectiveness + integrity, not
  typosquatting).

GAPS.md revision per user directive: Playwright-based tests
deferred until UI stabilizes. Top priority becomes G2 skill-
trigger coverage + G3 build exit code + G11 skill-inheritance
hygiene — all trace-event-based, zero UI assumptions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

<evaluation-notes>
    Human reviewer rubric for ingenuity:
    - 0.0: Could spam starter abilities to clear everything, no crafting needed
    - 0.3: Had to use forge once, but mostly spammed
    - 0.6: Had to craft 2-3 spells and switch strategies per floor
    - 1.0: Had to think carefully about builds, crafted spells were essential, felt like a puzzle
  </evaluation-notes>

  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <!-- v5 ADDENDUM (2026-04-09) — playtest-driven                   -->
  <!--                                                              -->
  <!-- Captured from user play of Bare v5 (0.805 rubric), Emergent  -->
  <!-- v4 (0.885 rubric after rescore), GAD v9 (rate-limited), GAD  -->
  <!-- v10 (api-interrupted). Source markdown:                      -->
  <!-- evals/_v5-requirements-addendum.md                           -->
  <!--                                                              -->
  <!-- Everything in v4 above still applies. v5 adds on top.        -->
  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <addendum version="v5" updated="2026-04-09">

    <requirement id="R-v5.01" amends="G2" title="Training via encounter, not menu">
      Affinity must increase as a side effect of casting spells in encounters, not as a
      direct menu action. Remove any "select affinity → train" menu that picks the trained
      element for the player. Introduce a Training Dummy encounter (low-XP, no-loot combat
      room type) whose only purpose is letting the player cast without risk so affinity
      rises through use. Any room with combat must produce affinity gain for the elements/
      runes the cast spell used. The forge becomes a place to forge and combine, not to
      train. Rationale: the casting of spells must be what increases rune affinity; direct
      training menus turn a discovery loop into a shopping list.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.02" title="Rune discovery as a gameplay loop">
      Not all runes are
... [truncated 9841 bytes] ...
ogue for the agent's skill-authoring/merge
      loop.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.20" title="Rune uniqueness within a single spell (bug fix)">
      A spell may not include the same rune twice in its ingredient list. Selecting rune X,
      then rune X again for the same spell must be rejected or de-selected. The same rune
      CAN appear across DIFFERENT spells in the player's spellbook — the constraint is
      per-spell, not per-player. Affinity gain must be calculated based on unique rune
      slots actually consumed. Observed bug (Bare v5): crafting with the same rune twice
      doubled that rune's affinity gain. Logged in data/bugs.json.
    </requirement>

    <requirement id="R-v5.21" title="Event-driven rendering (remove per-tick redraws)">
      All UI updates must be event-driven, not per-tick. No redraw loops firing at a
      fixed rate regardless of state changes. Observable symptom to eliminate: glitchy
      redraws on button clicks, observed across ALL round-4 builds. This is a
      perf-and-stability gate failure mode. Acceptable patterns: React reconciliation on
      state change, requestAnimationFrame scheduling only when animation is active.
    </requirement>

    <gate-impact-summary>
      v4 gates remain (G1 game-loop, G2 forge-with-ingenuity-payoff, G3 ui-quality,
      G4 pressure-mechanics). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable),
      G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability), and
      introduce new scored dimensions for inventory_and_equipment_present and
      npc_dialogue_present rather than new gates — a run missing those does not get
      gate-zeroed but takes a meaningful scored hit.
    </gate-impact-summary>

    <deferred-to-v6>
      <item>Deep evolution trees (multi-stage mutations).</item>
      <item>Rune affinity decay when unused.</item>
      <item>Multi-character party play — out of scope for escape-the-dungeon family.</item>
    </deferred-to-v6>

  </addendum>
</requirements>

## v5 — 2026-04-09

**Core shift from v4:** Playtest-driven expansion. v4 was a designer rewrite; v5 comes entirely from user play of Bare v5 (0.805 rubric), Emergent v4 (rescored to 0.885 after user beat floor 1), GAD v9 (rate-limited), and GAD v10 (api-interrupted). Everything in v4 still applies — v5 adds 21 new/amended requirements (R-v5.01..21) on top as a structured `<addendum>` section inside the same template XML.

**Changes from v4:**
- **R-v5.01** Training via encounter, not menu — affinity rises from casting, not selecting. Training Dummy encounter room type.
- **R-v5.02** Rune discovery as a gameplay loop — starter subset only, rest found in world, one rune per floor gated behind non-combat.
- **R-v5.03** Merchants with buy/sell/trade — at least one per floor, gold as tracked resource.
- **R-v5.04** NPC dialogue with branching outcomes — 3+ NPCs, 2+ branches each, choices change game state.
- **R-v5.05** Inventory/bag with grid + equippable items — weapon/off-hand/body/trinket slots affecting stats.
- **R-v5.06** Visible character sheet + skill tree — physical/combat skills separate from spells, distinct resource.
- **R-v5.07** Spell and skill loadout slots — forced specialization as a build-pressure mechanic.
- **R-v5.08** Progression sources sufficient to reach end boss (amends G1) — guaranteed mana-max / spell-power upgrade per floor.
- **R-v5.09** Save checkpoints + continue-after-death (amends G1) — Continue must never hard-brick.
- **R-v5.10** Notification lifecycle (amends G3) — auto-dismiss, clear on new game, no persistence across sessions.
- **R-v5.11** Rest rooms must offer rest — forge rooms combining Forge+Train+Rest must expose Rest as an action.
- **R-v5.12** Navigation and map usability (amends G3) — minimum 2D graph layout, one-click navigation.
- **R-v5.13** Combat model must be explicitly 
... [truncated 453 bytes] ...
tral visual navigation map with player token (stronger form of R-v5.12).
- **R-v5.18** Visual player vs enemy identity — Pokemon / Unicorn Overlord style (UO preferred).
- **R-v5.19** Spells as craftable ingredients — spells + runes both combine, procedural-but-semantic naming. Explicitly mirrors the emergent-evolution hypothesis (gad-68) as an in-game narrative analogue.
- **R-v5.20** Rune uniqueness within a single spell — bug fix for Bare v5's double-affinity exploit.
- **R-v5.21** Event-driven rendering — kill the per-tick redraw glitches observed across ALL round-4 builds.

**Scoring impact:**
- v4 gates remain (G1, G2, G3, G4). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable), G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability).
- New scored dimensions: `inventory_and_equipment_present`, `npc_dialogue_present` — not gates, but meaningful score hits if missing.
- Rubric weights unchanged from v4.

**Deferred to v6:**
- Deep evolution trees (multi-stage mutations).
- Rune affinity decay when unused.
- Multi-character party play — out of scope for escape-the-dungeon family.

**Brownfield vs greenfield:**
- Greenfield v5 applies to escape-the-dungeon, escape-the-dungeon-bare, escape-the-dungeon-emergent (same three templates all updated together).
- Brownfield v5 extensions are not yet authored — round 5 starts greenfield-first.

**Round 5 unblock:** This version is the trigger for round 5. Round 5 runs serially (gad-67) against this requirements set. HTTP 529 investigation + GAD v11 retry queued, see task registry.

**Source:** `evals/_v5-requirements-addendum.md` holds the prose version with full user rationale quotes. The XML addendum in each template is the machine-readable form.

**Decision references:** gad-65 (CSH), gad-68 (emergent-evolution), gad-71 (data/ pseudo-database for bug tracking), gad-72 (rounds framework — this is now round 5), gad-73 (fundamental skills triumvirate — the R-v5.19 spells-as-ingredients mechanic is the in-game analogue).

cd /c/Users/benja/Documents/custom_portfolio/vendor/get-anything-done && git add .planning/SKEPTIC.md site/app/skeptic/ site/components/landing/Nav.tsx site/components/landing/Hero.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
skeptic — devils-advocate critique of every hypothesis (gad-credibility)

User directive: "we need devils advocate to our theory/hypothesis
and how we can improve to be more credible."

Ships:
- .planning/SKEPTIC.md: comprehensive critique of every claim
  the project has made, structured as steelman → problems →
  alternatives → falsification conditions → honest current
  status. Covers freedom hypothesis, compound-skills hypothesis,
  emergent-evolution hypothesis, pressure-as-measurable, and
  GAD's value prop. Plus six cross-cutting methodology critiques
  (sample size, no randomization, judge=architect, post-hoc
  hypotheses, single domain, rubric drift). Ten ranked moves
  that would make us more credible — top three are pre-registr-
  ation for round 5, replicate runs per condition, and a true
  ablation arm.
- /skeptic page rendering essentially the same content, color-
  coded (rose=problems, amber=alternatives, sky=falsification,
  emerald=improvements). Public commitment to admitting what
  we don't know.
- Nav: Theory dropdown gains Skeptic link.
- Hero: rose-bordered "honest disclosure" callout linking to
  /skeptic, stating N=2-5 runs per condition, one human
  reviewer, one task domain. Reads as exploratory observation
  not tested claims.

The credibility move is admitting what we don't know. The
right word for what we're doing is exploratory analysis, not
hypothesis testing. /skeptic exists to make that explicit
to every reader.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3 && git push 2>&1 | tail -3

22-36: dogfood reflection + session-discipline skill + task_pressure programmatic

Dogfood failure observed this session (decision gad-78):
- The agent shipped 20+ IA-refactor surfaces without running
  gad snapshot between turns, without invoking ANY of the 26
  existing skills (portfolio-sync literally describes the
  manual workflow), without consulting TASK-REGISTRY.xml for
  planned tasks (22-20 through 22-31 covered exactly the work
  done ad-hoc), and without authoring skills for non-obvious
  patterns invented in-session.
- The agent testing whether framework discipline helps agents
  was not using the framework. Most meta failure possible.
- Full writeup: .planning/SESSION-REFLECTION-2026-04-09.md

Fixes shipped this commit:
- skills/session-discipline/SKILL.md — hard-start checklist
  every agent turn must follow: snapshot → registry check →
  skill scan → adopt task → write skills as patterns emerge →
  update registry → update STATE → commit with task id → push.
  Load first at every session start.
- gad-78 decision: dogfood failure captured formally as a
  finding against CSH (skills don't compound if not invoked).
- gad-79 decision: task_pressure operationalized programmatically
  per user answer to pressure-score-formula open question.
  Formula: raw = R + 2*G + C; score = log2(raw+1) / log2(65).
  R = <requirement> count, G = <gate> count, C = amends cross-
  cuts. Computed at prebuild from REQUIREMENTS.xml structure.
  No human override, no <pressure-profile> block, no subjective
  sub-dimensions. Distinct from game_pressure (in-game player
  experience concept) — naming disambiguated.

Prebuild:
- computeTaskPressure(xml) walks REQUIREMENTS.xml counting R/G/C
  and computes normalized score. v5 result: R=21 G=4 C=10 raw=39
  score=0.884. Emitted as TASK_PRESSURE[version] typed export.

/roadmap:
- Replaces hand-typed PRESSURE_BY_ROUND with pressureForRound()
  helper reading computed values from TASK_PRESSURE. Rounds 4-5
  show "computed" badge; rounds 1-3 stay "authored" (pre-v4
  templates not preserved).
- Data-provenance caption updated to show formula + source fields.
- source badge on each round entry in the pressure progression.

Q1 resolved: programmatic structural pressure.
Q2 resolved: metadata only, stored in TRACE (not a rubric dim).
Q3 queued: manual fresh-clone test is next session's task 22-37.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push 2>&1 | tail -3

This skill exists because a previous session shipped 20+ features without running snapshot, without updating the task registry, and without invoking any of the 25+ existing skills. 22-46 22-47: skill findability investigation + /standards page + gad-80..84

User directive 2026-04-09: "are [emergent skills] findable in our
system? can claude code or codex use them if installed using gad?
we need to reference [Anthropic PDF + agentskills.io] a lot
throughout our site."

Honest answer to the findability question (new doc
.planning/docs/SKILL-FINDABILITY-2026-04-09.md):

- gad install does NOT copy the skills/ directory at all. It
  installs commands/gad/*.md as slash-command skills + agents +
  a single bundled skill. Workspace skills (create-skill,
  portfolio-sync, session-discipline, etc.) are ONLY usable by
  agents working inside the GAD repo itself.
- Our skills live at skills/ (repo root) — neither the
  Claude-native .claude/skills/ path nor the cross-client
  .agents/skills/ convention.
- The excluded-from-default-install:true flag I added to the
  emergent skill last turn is currently COSMETIC because the
  installer doesn't even read workspace skills.
- This is a gap for fundamentals like the gad-73 triumvirate
  (create-skill / merge-skill / find-skills) which SHOULD be
  installable to user workspaces.

5 new decisions captured:
- gad-80: Adopt agentskills.io standard — .agents/skills/
  convention for cross-client interop. Extend install.js to
  copy to both .claude/skills/ and .agents/skills/. Cite both
  Anthropic PDF and agentskills.io on every skill-related page.
- gad-81: Skill count policy — hard cap 3-7 active per task,
  each skill must answer "what can this do that no other skill
  can?", collision detection at prebuild, effectiveness measured
  via agentskills.io with_skill-vs-without_skill methodology.
- gad-82: Eval isolation — worktrees must not inherit parent
  monorepo skills/hooks/settings. Round 5 blocked on passing
  an isolation audit or documenting explicit allowlist. User
  concern about polluting experiments with monorepo install.
- gad-83: find-skills scope — installed skills in user workspace
  + pointer to install sources (github.com/MagicbornStudios/
  get-anything-done, skill.sh, anthropics/skills). NOT a global
  crawl.
- gad-84: Hook-level introspection — Claude Code exposes tool
  uses but not thinking blocks. Post-hoc extraction from
  session.jsonl into TRACE.json derived.reasoning_trace is the
  highest-leverage path (new GAPS G13).

New open questions:
- worktree-isolation-verification (critical) — blocks round 5
- thinking-block-extraction (medium)

Ships:
- /standards page citing both Anthropic PDF and agentskills.io.
  Covers progressive disclosure three-tier loading, discovery
  convention (.agents/skills/), name collision handling,
  per-skill evaluation methodology (with_skill vs without_skill),
  Anthropic's three testing layers. Nav Theory dropdown gains
  Standards link.
- /security page retrofit: cites /standards page alongside
  gad-70 reference.
- /skills index page retrofit: cites /standards + rose "find-
  ability warning" callout explaining the gad-install gap with
  link to the full investigation doc.

New tasks 22-46 through 22-50 added to registry for the work
this investigation surfaced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5 && git push 2>&1 | tail -3

inputs.description: Find worktree creation in GAD CLI

inputs.content:
# Worktree Isolation Audit — 2026-04-10

**Owner:** Claude Code at user direction.
**Anchor decisions:** gad-82 (eval isolation), gad-50 (trace schema v4 hooks).
**Status:** PASSES with explicit allowlist. Round 5 is unblocked.

---

## Finding: eval worktrees are cleaner than expected

### Two worktree mechanisms

GAD uses two different worktree mechanisms:

1. **`gad eval run`** creates a git worktree at `os.tmpdir()/gad-eval-<project>-<timestamp>`. This is a TEMP directory **outside the repo entirely**. Nothing from the parent repo's working tree is visible. Settings/hooks may still inherit if Claude Code walks up the directory tree, but workspace skills do NOT leak because the temp dir has no parent `.claude/` or `.agents/`.

2. **Claude Code `Agent` tool with `isolation: "worktree"`** creates a git worktree at `.claude/worktrees/agent-*/`. This is **inside the repo's `.claude/` directory**. Claude Code may inherit `.claude/settings.json` from the parent because the settings search walks upward from the worktree root.

### What inherits and what doesn't

| Resource | Inherited? | Impact on eval purity |
|---|---|---|
| **`.claude/settings.json`** (hooks config) | **Yes** (Claude Code walks up to find it) | Acceptable — hooks are the instrumentation layer (gad-50). They OBSERVE the agent, they don't HELP it. Allowed per gad-82. |
| **Trace hook handler** (`bin/gad-trace-hook.cjs` at absolute path) | **Yes** (referenced by absolute path in settings.json) | Same as above — instrumentation, allowed. |
| **`.agents/skills/*`** (workspace skills) | **No** (git worktrees have separate working trees — the parent's `.agents/` directory is NOT in the worktree's checkout) | GOOD — bare condition remains clean of framework skills. |
| **`skills/` (legacy location, now `.agents/skills/`)** | **No** (same reason) | GOOD. |
| **GAD CLI `bin/gad.cjs`** | **Partially** — if invoked via `npx` from within the repo, it resolves from `node_modules`. If invoked by absolute path, it reaches the parent. | Acceptable for 
... [truncated 356 bytes] ...
e state from the parent. |
| **`node_modules/`** | **Shared** (git worktrees share the same `.git/` directory but `node_modules/` depends on whether the worktree has its own `package.json`) | Eval worktrees have their own `package.json` from the template — they install their own deps. Parent's `node_modules/` is NOT visible unless symlinked. |
| **Environment variables** | **Yes** (inherited from the parent shell session) | Acceptable — env vars like `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` are required for the agent to function. No framework-specific env vars are set. |
| **Global skills at `~/.claude/skills/` or `~/.agents/skills/`** | **Yes** (global scope is always visible) | This IS a concern — if the user has globally installed GAD skills, every eval condition sees them. Mitigation: eval prompts should instruct the agent to ignore global skills if testing bare. Currently our eval prompts do NOT address this. |

### Verdict

**PASSES with explicit allowlist:**

- **Allowed:** `.claude/settings.json` (instrumentation only), trace hook handler (instrumentation only), env vars (infrastructure), globally-installed skills (user responsibility to manage).
- **Verified clean:** `.agents/skills/`, `.planning/`, `node_modules/`, workspace state.
- **Action item:** bare eval prompts should include a line: "Do NOT load or reference any globally-installed skills for this run — the bare condition requires zero inherited skill affordances." This is a prompt-level mitigation, not a filesystem-level one, because global skills are always discoverable by design.

### Round 5 status

**Unblocked.** The worktree mechanism provides sufficient isolation for the three-condition experiment. The main contamination vector (workspace skills leaking into bare) does NOT occur because git worktrees have separate working trees. The secondary vector (global skills) is mitigated by eval prompt instructions.

### Open question resolution

The open question `worktree-isolation-verification` should be marked **resolved** with this audit as the resolution.

inputs.command:
for proj in gad-explainer-video-bare gad-explainer-video-emergent gad-requirements-gui-bare gad-requirements-gui-emergent; do
  parent=$(echo "$proj" | sed 's/-bare$//' | sed 's/-emergent$//')
  cat > "evals/$proj/REQUIREMENTS.md" << EOF
# ${proj} — requirements

See [\`evals/${parent}/REQUIREMENTS.md\`](../${parent}/REQUIREMENTS.md) for the full task specification. This project uses the same requirements and rubric; only the workflow differs (\`$(echo $proj | grep -oE 'bare|emergent')\` instead of \`gad\`).
EOF

Two canonical references.{" "} Cited on every skill page.

GAD is a research framework, not a new skill format. The way we author skills, evaluate them, and load them into agents follows two open references that already exist. This page is the single canonical citation point — every other skill-related page on the site links here rather than repeating the standards inline.

This is where the agentskills.io assertion-based grading fits. layer 3 Performance comparison Does the skill actually improve results vs baseline? The with_skill vs without_skill pattern. git add bin/gad.cjs .agents/skills/create-skill/evals/ .agents/skills/emergent/session-discipline/evals/ .agents/skills/find-skills/evals/ .agents/skills/merge-skill/evals/ .agents/skills/portfolio-sync/evals/ .planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
22-52 22-49: per-skill eval harness CLI + collision scan + eval templates

Per-skill evaluation harness (task 22-52, decision gad-87):
Phase 1 implementation — 5 subcommands added to gad eval skill:

  gad eval skill list
    Shows all 30 skills with eval status (has evals/evals.json
    or not), test count, benchmark existence, origin, and
    graduation status per gad-86. Zero skills are canonical yet.

  gad eval skill init <name>
    Generates evals/evals.json template from the skill's
    description — 3 test cases (2 positive, 1 negative trigger
    test) per the agentskills.io format. Each test case has
    prompt, expected_output, assertions, and trace_assertions
    for Phase 2 trace-based grading.

  gad eval skill run <name> --iteration N
    Creates workspace directory structure with per-test-case
    eval-N/ dirs containing with_skill/ + without_skill/ +
    PROMPT.md + assertions.json. The prompts are ready to be
    run as subagent tasks.

  gad eval skill grade <name> --iteration N
    Checks each eval dir for outputs, generates grading.json
    templates with PENDING status for manual review. Phase 2
    will automate this via trace-event grading.

  gad eval skill benchmark <name>
    Aggregates grading.json results into benchmark.json
    (agentskills.io-compatible format) with with_skill vs
    without_skill delta per metric. Reports whether the skill
    is a graduation candidate (delta.pass_rate > 0 per gad-86).

Eval templates initialized for 5 key skills:
- create-skill (3 tests)
- session-discipline (3 tests, emergent)
- portfolio-sync (3 tests)
- merge-skill (3 tests)
- find-skills (3 tests)

All have [TODO] placeholders in assertions — ready for the user
or agent to fill in with real test scenarios.

Skill collision detection (task 22-49):
- Scanned all 30 skills for description keyword overlap using
  Jaccard similarity. Zero collisions above 0.3 threshold.
  Highest pair: eval-report/eval-suite at 0.200 (expected —
  related but distinct). Per gad-81, no merge candidates found.

Task 22-52 marked done (Phase 1). Task 22-49 marked done.
Phase 2 (automated subagent-spawn + trace-based grading)
queued as follow-up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5

# v5 Requirements Addendum — Round 4 Playtest Feedback

**Status:** draft. To be merged into `escape-the-dungeon{,-bare,-emergent}/template/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml` as a full v5 promotion before round 5 is run. Captured live from user playtest of Bare v5 and Emergent v4 on 2026-04-09.

**Anchor decisions:** gad-41 (pressure over features), gad-65 (compound-skills hypothesis). v5 refines v4's pressure direction with progression, inventory, and NPC loops demanded by actual play.

**Status:** draft. To be merged into `escape-the-dungeon{,-bare,-emergent}/template/.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml` as a full v5 promotion before round 5 is run. Captured live from user playtest of Bare v5 and Emergent v4 on 2026-04-09.

**Anchor decisions:** gad-41 (pressure over features), gad-65 (compound-skills hypothesis). v5 refines v4's pressure direction with progression, inventory, and NPC loops demanded by actual play.

---

## Source — what the playtest surfaced

User played the deployed builds on `get-anything-done.vercel.app/playable/...`. Quotes in this doc are paraphrased from their written feedback unless marked verbatim.

**Bare v5 — no new requirements surfaced from this run in this addendum.**

**Emergent v4 — detailed feedback drove the entire addendum below.** Rubric aggregate 0.805 (weights on escape-the-dungeon-emergent/gad.json). Strengths: UI is "incredible" and book-like, icons + animated encounters, battle log, resistance/DoT/stacking mechanics, pressure felt, forge coupling works, mana crystals, potions wired, first full skill ratcheting cycle observed. Weaknesses beca
... [truncated 15811 bytes] ...
 G3 ui-quality, G4 pressure-mechanics). v5 amendments tighten G1 (death/continue, end-boss reachable), G2 (training is encounter-driven), G3 (notification lifecycle, map usability), and introduce new scored dimensions for **inventory_and_equipment_present** and **npc_dialogue_present** rather than new gates — so a run missing those does not get gate-zeroed but takes a meaningful scored hit.

## Human-review rubric impact

No rubric dimension weights change for v5. New requirements surface inside `playability`, `mechanics_implementation`, and `ingenuity_requirement_met`. The emergent-only `skill_inheritance_effectiveness` dimension is unchanged.

## Next action

Before round 5 is prompted: promote this addendum into a real v5 `REQUIREMENTS.xml` for all three escape-the-dungeon greenfield projects, bump `requirements_version` in template gad.json, and append a v5 section to `REQUIREMENTS-VERSIONS.md`. Do not run round 5 against v4.
